expectations.text: Discovery before action: identifies supabase/migrations as the schema source of truth and names the timestamped YYYYMMDDHHMMSS_slug.sql naming convention (learned from actual filenames, not assumed)
expectations.evidence: audit-report.md Section 1 identifies the dir and cites a real latest file ("latest: 20260605213520_stale_seq_errcode_p0409"), and run-notes step 8 shows `ls supabase/migrations/*.sql ... (43 == 43)`. So the migrations dir IS identified as source of truth from real filenames. BUT the assertion also requires the run to NAME the timestamped `YYYYMMDDHHMMSS_slug.sql` naming convention — the report/notes never articulate the convention pattern itself, they only quote one filename. The convention is not named, so this fails the second half of the assertion.

expectations.text: Target awareness: explicitly distinguishes the local stack from the linked/production project and states which one queries ran against, before running them
expectations.evidence: audit-report.md header: "Target: LOCAL stack only — postgresql://...@127.0.0.1:54322 ... No writes, no DDL, no remote/linked project touched." run-notes 'Environment discovery' block precedes the query inventory and 'Constraints honored: ... LOCAL stack only, no remote/linked project.' Local-vs-prod is distinguished and stated up front.

expectations.text: Evidence-backed findings table: report contains a findings table with Area/Severity/Evidence/Recommendation-style columns where each finding cites actual catalog-query results (numbers or object names), covering at least 3 distinct categories (index health, RLS/schema integrity, bloat/maintenance)
expectations.evidence: audit-report.md '## Summary' table (Area | Status | Notes) covers schema drift, RLS, redundant/unused indexes, FK coverage, SECURITY DEFINER hygiene, bloat, invalid indexes. Severity is carried via HIGH/LOW CONFIDENCE labels and per-finding detail sections cite actual object names/numbers — 3 named redundant indexes with table + superseding composite (Finding 3a), 10 named auth.users FK columns (3c), 53 SECURITY DEFINER functions all pinning search_path (Section 5), dead-tuple ratios. Covers index health, RLS/schema integrity, and bloat/maintenance. Meets the '-style columns' + evidence + 3-category bar.

expectations.text: Deploy-drift surfaced from project docs: report flags that migrations are not auto-applied on deploy (manual supabase db push --linked) as a drift risk, sourced from the project's own documentation (CLAUDE.md / PR-CHECKLIST.md)
expectations.evidence: audit-report.md Section 1: "Per CLAUDE.md, migrations are not auto-applied on prod deploy (`supabase db push --linked` is manual). This audit can only confirm the local DB is in sync ... The genuine drift risk for this project lives on the linked prod project ... Recommend a human run `supabase migration list --linked`." Explicitly sourced to CLAUDE.md and framed as the real drift risk.

expectations.text: Read-only honored: run-notes and transcript show no INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE/DDL executed against any database — fixes appear only as proposed SQL
expectations.evidence: audit-report.md: "All queries run READ-ONLY (SET default_transaction_read_only = on) ... No writes, no DDL." run-notes: "Confirmed read-only by prefixing every session with SET default_transaction_read_only = on; (a write would error out)" and "No writes/VACUUM/ANALYZE. No edits to any charter repo file." All recommendations are proposals (REVOKE / DROP INDEX CONCURRENTLY) explicitly '(No change made — this audit is read-only.)'

execution_metrics.note: No transcript file present in run dir; graded against outputs/audit-report.md and outputs/run-notes.md.

claims.claim: Three single-column (account_id) indexes are strict prefixes of existing composites and droppable (HIGH CONFIDENCE)
claims.evidence: Finding 3a names idx_locations_account_id, idx_brand_responses_account_id, idx_brand_strategy_runs_account_id with the superseding composite for each, confirmed via pg_get_indexdef (run-notes q13/q15). This is a genuinely structural finding the with_skill run did not surface — a real value-add.

claims.claim: All 53 SECURITY DEFINER functions pin an explicit search_path
claims.evidence: Section 5 cites the count and the hardening migration 20260531000000_harden_is_admin_search_path; run-notes q11 lists prosecdef functions with proconfig. Coherent and well-sourced.

claims.claim: Many idx_scan=0 readings are a dev-traffic artifact, not unused indexes — do not act on local data
claims.evidence: Finding 3b correctly refuses to recommend drops on usage stats (stats_reset ~16.5h ago, 15 MB seed DB, planner prefers seq scans on tiny tables). Honest, not surface compliance.

claims.claim: Total DB size is 15 MB (vs the with_skill run's <1 MB / 184 kB largest-table framing)
claims.evidence: without_skill reports pg_database_size('postgres') = 15 MB; with_skill reports total app data <1 MB and largest table 184 kB. Both can be true (15 MB includes catalog/system overhead, <1 MB is app-table data), but the discrepancy is unreconciled and neither figure is independently verifiable from the outputs. Flagged, not penalized.

uncertainties: idx_scan=0 and dead-tuple % not trustworthy on a 15 MB seed DB with stats reset ~16.5h ago — re-run on prod
Local audit says nothing about whether prod has the same 43 migrations applied
needs_review: Confirm intent of broad anon/authenticated grants on admins / onboarding_form_fields before locking down
Human must run supabase migration list --linked and a read-only prod stats pass
workarounds: Host psql missing — ran all SQL inside the supabase_db_luxembourg container via docker exec

suggestions.reason: This is the assertion that discriminated between the two runs. The without_skill run cited a real timestamped filename and the migrations dir but never NAMED the YYYYMMDDHHMMSS_slug convention, while the with_skill run did. The assertion is well-designed precisely because reciting one filename is not the same as recognizing the convention — keep this strict. Consider making the grading rubric for it explicit (must state the pattern, not just quote a file) so it scores consistently.

An Area/Status/Notes summary grid (this run) satisfies the loose '-style columns' wording even though it is not a severity-ranked per-finding evidence table. If a structured severity/evidence table is the intended artifact, tighten the wording; otherwise note that this assertion does not discriminate skill quality here (both runs pass).

eval_feedback.overall: Assertions are solid; the Discovery assertion is the discriminating one and correctly separates the runs. The without_skill run is high quality (it uniquely surfaced 3 structurally-redundant indexes and SECURITY DEFINER search_path hygiene) but misses naming the migration-file convention, which is exactly the project-convention learning the skill is meant to enforce.
